scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff states that he is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} by profession and the Defendant is a {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} by profession. The parties are known to each other and have been on friendly terms prior to the transaction herein.

6. In March 2024, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff requesting financial assistance by way of a hand loan for his personal requirements. Acting on the faith and trust reposed in the Defendant, the Plaintiff agreed to advance the sum without insisting on a formal written loan agreement.

7. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff transferred a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant's bank account via NEFT. The transaction is evidenced by the bank transfer receipt bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456, marked as Annexure A.

8. The Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said amount and the liability to repay the same through electronic messages on WhatsApp. The relevant extracts of the WhatsApp conversations dated {{WHATSAPP_MESSAGE_DATE}} wherein the Defendant admitted the debt stating {{WHATSAPP_MESSAGE_CONTENT}} are marked as Annexure B.

9. The loan was advanced on the oral understanding that the same would be repaid on or before 20.06.2024. The Plaintiff further states that he is not a habitual money lender and the said amount was advanced out of his own spare funds as a friendly assistance.

BREACH AND PARTICULARS OF DEFAULT

10. Despite the agreed date of repayment having arrived on 20.06.2024, the Defendant failed to repay the principal amount or any part thereof. The Plaintiff made repeated oral requests and demands for repayment, but the Defendant postponed the same on various pretexts.

11. Subsequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 through his Advocate calling upon the Defendant to repay the outstanding dues. The Defendant received the said notice but has {{LEGAL_NOTICE_REPLY_STATUS}}. The copy of the legal notice and postal acknowledgment are marked as Annexure C.

12. Despite the expiry of the time granted in the legal notice, the Defendant has failed, neglected, and refused to repay the loan amount, thereby committing a breach of the obligation to repay the debt.

DAMAGES

13. By reason of the breach committed by the Defendant, the Plaintiff is entitled to recover the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- along with interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024, till the date of realisation.

14. The interest claimed is reasonable and within the permissible limits for unsecured loans between individuals, and the Defendant is liable to pay the same in addition to the principal amount.

15. The total claim comprises the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- and accrued interest calculated at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 until the date of payment, besides the costs of this suit.

LEGAL BASIS

16. The Defendant borrowed / received a sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- from the Plaintiff and is liable to repay the same with interest under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

17. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

19. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite
